reason: This is a high-quality sample with a coherent task blueprint that is well-reflected in the trajectory. The assistant uses tools appropriately (step, formula, practice) and provides accurate, well-grounded mathematical explanations. The user behaves like a realistic student: polite, methodical, progressing from confusion to confidence, asking appropriate follow-up questions. All four blueprint goals are meaningfully pursued and completed. Minor issues include the practice tool output containing some internal processing artifacts (visible in the function response), but this doesn't materially damage the sample since the assistant presents clean, usable problems to the user. The namespace prefixes on tool names (skill-tools-*) are harmless runtime artifacts that don't affect quality.
